title: Caffeine in Foods - Canada.ca
content:
- Recommended maximum daily intake
- Amounts in food and drink
Caffeine is a natural part of:
- flavourings and extracts, such as: kola guarana yerba mate
Some foods are also allowed to contain added caffeine. Health Canada regulates the addition of caffeine to foods.

## Recommended maximum daily intake
Health Canada scientists have reviewed several studies about caffeine and its potential health effects. Based on this review, we recommend maximum daily caffeine intakes depending on your age and circumstances.
Some of the side effects of consuming caffeine may include:
If you're sensitive to caffeine, consider avoiding it or lowering your intake.
Recommended maximum daily intake
Adults (18 years and over)
People planning to become pregnant
People who are pregnant
People who are breastfeeding
Children and adolescents (up to 18 years) Footnote *
2.5 mg per kg of body weight
Footnote * This RMDI is based on limited data available for children. Health Canada applied the RMDI for children to adolescents because of insufficient data for adolescents, but this is a precautionary approach. Health Canada recognizes that healthy older and heavier adolescents (i.e., 14-18 years of age) may be able to consume caffeine in a similar manner to healthy adults. Return to footnote * referrer

Health Canada applied the RMDI for children to adolescents because of insufficient data for adolescents, but this is a precautionary approach. Health Canada recognizes that healthy older and heavier adolescents (i.e., 14-18 years of age) may be able to consume caffeine in a similar manner to healthy adults.

## Amounts in food and drink
Different foods contain different amounts of caffeine on average. You can use this table to help follow Health Canada's recommendations. You can also check food labels as some may tell you if the product contains caffeine and how much.
Serving size (unless otherwise stated)
Average amount of caffeine
Roasted and ground, percolated
Roasted and ground, filter drip
Roasted and ground, decaffeinated
Instant decaffeinated
Black or green, brewed
Cola beverage, regular
1 envelope hot-cocoa mix
Candy, milk chocolate
Candy, sweet chocolate
Baking chocolate, unsweetened
- List of Permitted Food Additives With Other Accepted Uses
